setting.note: Gilded-Age Washington seen from a hired house on Lafayette Square, across from the White House. Adams maps the real capital from the inside: the Senate gallery, the presidential reception, and the steamer excursion down the Potomac to Mount Vernon. He published anonymously and knew every one of these rooms.

rights.originalWork: Public domain. Henry Adams, first published anonymously in 1880; the author died in 1918.
rights.territoryNotes: Public domain in the United States (published 1880), and in the United Kingdom and the European Union, where the author died more than 70 years ago (1918).
rights.attribution: Henry Adams, Democracy: An American Novel (1880). Text from Project Gutenberg.

palette.note: The blue-and-gold of a clear Washington day, drawn straight from the text. A deep slate blue that clears WCAG AA on paper.
